description_long: This object is a rectangular stone block with a series of evenly spaced, parallel grooves running across its top surface, creating a striped pattern. The grooves are deep and regular, giving the block a distinctive ribbed appearance. The stone appears to be a dark, possibly granite or basalt material, with a slightly mottled texture visible in the lighter areas between the grooves. The sides of the block are smooth and unadorned, contrasting with the textured top. The block is solid and heavy, likely used as a barrier or obstacle in urban environments such as sidewalks, plazas, or parking lots. There are no visible signs of significant weathering, engravings, or additional features.
description: A dark, rectangular stone block with deep, parallel grooves across the top, creating a ribbed, striped appearance. The sides are smooth and the material appears dense and heavy.
description_view_0: Front view showing the grooved top surface and the smooth, solid front face.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the depth and regularity of the grooves, with the smooth side face visible.
description_view_2: Rear view similar to the front, displaying the grooved top and plain back face.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again emphasizing the deep grooves and the smooth, unmarked side.
attribute_location_context: urban sidewalk or plaza as a barrier or seating element
attribute_installation_method: embedded in concrete or set on a prepared foundation
attribute_weathering_pattern: No significant weathering visible; surface appears clean with minor mottling.
attribute_integration_with_surrounding_s: isolated or aligned in a row with similar blocks
